project_type: Python trading bot - CCT Rectangle Strategy with backtesting, adding live MT5 trading
test_framework: No formal tests - manual validation via running the bot
test_command: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'CCT_Rectangle_Bot/live_mt5'); import mt5_config; import risk_manager; import logger_setup; import dashboard; import mt5_data_feed; import mt5_connector; import mt5_trader; print('All modules import successfully')"
verification_instructions: Verify all files exist, import cleanly (since MT5 is Windows-only, we validate imports with conditional MetaTrader5 import), and have proper structure
setup_instructions: pip install -r CCT_Rectangle_Bot/live_mt5/requirements.txt (on Windows with MT5 installed)
environment_constraints: MetaTrader5 Python package only works on Windows. Cannot run live tests in this sandbox. Validation limited to syntax/import checks and code review.
contribution_requirements: Files go in CCT_Rectangle_Bot/live_mt5/ directory. Use conservative defaults (1-2% risk). Reuse strategy classes from parent directory.
key_patterns: Strategy uses DataFrames with columns: Open, High, Low, Close, Volume. DirectionDetector takes df_4h, WeaknessDetector takes df_15m, RectangleEntry takes df_1m and df_15m. CCTRectangleStrategy orchestrates all three. Config module pattern with uppercase constants. Uses dataclasses for signals and trade setups.
directory_structure: CCT_Rectangle_Bot/ contains the backtest bot. live_mt5/ subdirectory will contain all live trading files. Strategy classes in strategy.py (DirectionDetector, WeaknessDetector, RectangleEntry, CCTRectangleStrategy) and utilities in utils.py will be imported by the live trader.
